zh.Greyfriars Church, Reading, Berkshire, E: 英國波克夏雷亞丁格也福利亞斯 邱奇
zh.lifeSummary: Margaret Drew是Thomas Drew和Agnes的女兒，於大約1425年出生在英國。她於大約1449年與Walter Samborne在英國結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。她去世於1495年2月14日在英國波克夏素可特享年71歲，埋葬在英國波克夏雷亞丁格也福利亞斯 邱奇。

zh-hans.Greyfriars Church, Reading, Berkshire, E: 英国波克夏雷亞丁格也福利亞斯 邱奇
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Margaret Drew是Thomas Drew和Agnes的女儿，于大约1425年出生在英国。她于大约1449年与Walter Samborne在英国结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。她去世于1495年2月14日在英国波克夏素可特享年71岁，埋葬在英国波克夏雷亞丁格也福利亞斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Drew
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from a Norman French personal name Dreus based on ancient Germanic Drogo, the name of one of the sons of Charlemagne (compare Gothic driugan ‘to serve as a soldier’). In Old French this name became Dreus, Drues (nominative) and Dru, Driu, Dreu (accusative) and was introduced to England by the Normans. For further information about this name, see also Montague .
English: from the personal name Drew, a pet form of Andrew which was confused with the same name of Old French origin (see 1 above).

Irish: when not an adoption of the English surname, a shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac an Druaidh or Ó Druaidh or Ó Draoi ‘son’ and ‘descendant of the druid’, from draoi ‘druid’, genitive druadh or draoi. All of these were often Anglicized as Drury .

variants.name: Drews
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Gerhard, Gunter, Hans, Heinz, Helmut, Otto, Reinhold, Bernd, Detlef, Egon, Erna.
English: variant of Drew with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is also found in Guernsey in the Channel Islands.
Americanized form of German, Danish, and Dutch   Drewes  .

variants.name: Drewer
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Old French personal name Droart, Drouart (which is probably from ancient Germanic Droghard, with the first element as in Drew + hard ‘brave, hardy’).

variants.name: Drewett
meanings: English (Wiltshire, Berkshire, and Surrey): of Norman origin, from the Old French personal name Druet, a diminutive of Drue, Dreu (from ancient Germanic Drogo); see Drew 1. Alternatively, the name may be from a diminutive of Old French dru ‘lover’; see Drew 4.

variants.name: Dronet
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Alcee, Desire, Olympe.

French (mainly Loire-Atlantique): from a diminutive of the personal name Dron, derived from the ancient Germanic name Drogo, a short form of compound names based on the element drog, presumably meaning ‘battle’ (see also Drew ).

variants.name: Droz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Aida, Cruz Maria, Diego, Jose, Miguel, Milagros, Nydia, Osvaldo, Rodriguez, Vidal.

French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Drogo, a short form of compound names based on the element drog, presumably meaning ‘battle’ (see also Drew ). This surname is also found in Puerto Rico.

variants.name: Drea
meanings: Irish (Kilkenny): shortened Anglicized form of Ó Draoi (see Drew ).

variants.name: Montague
meanings: English (of Norman origin): habitational name from either Montaigu-le-bois (Manche) or Montaigu (Manche, Aisne), named with Old French mont ‘hill’ + agu ‘pointed’ (from Latin acutus). Drogo de Montagud was a companion of William the Conqueror and founder of the Montagu(e) family, among whom the personal name Drogo was revived in the 19th century. Another family of the name Monatgu have held the titles of Duke of Montagu, Earl of Sandwich, Earl and Duke of Manchester, Earl of Halifax, and Earl of Beaulieu, and.
Irish: adopted as equivalent of Gaelic Mac Taidhg (see McTigue ).

variants.name: Drouin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Marcel, Armand, Benoit, Jacques, Andre, Clermont, Emile, Gaston, Gilles, Ovide, Pierre, Serge.

French: from a pet form of the Old French personal name Droue, a variant of Dreue, which is from the ancient Germanic name Drogo, a short form of compound names based on the element drog, presumably meaning ‘battle’ (see also Drew ). Compare Derouen , Derouin , and Tron .

variants.name: Drury
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Drury  .

variants.name: Drouhard
meanings: French:
from the ancient Germanic personal name Droghard, composed of the elements drog (presumably meaning ‘battle’; see also Drew ) and hard ‘hard, strong’.
alternatively, a derivative of the ancient Germanic name Drogo, a short form of compound names like Droghard (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: Margaret Drew was born about 1425, in England as the daughter of Thomas Drew and Agnes. She married Walter Samborne about 1449, in England. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. She died on 14 February 1495, in Southcote, Berkshire, England, at the age of 71, and was buried in Greyfriars Church, Reading, Berkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Margaret Drew est née environ 1425, à Angleterre, fille de Thomas Drew et Agnes. Elle a épousé Walter Samborne environ 1449, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Elle est décédée le 14 février 1495, à Southcote, Berkshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 71 ans, et a été enterrée à Greyfriars Church, Reading, Berkshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Margaret Drew wurde um 1425 in England, als Tochter von Thomas Drew und Agnes geboren. Sie hat um 1449 in England Walter Samborne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Sie starb am 14. Februar 1495 im Alter von 71 Jahren in Southcote, Berkshire, England und wurde in Greyfriars Church, Reading, Berkshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Margaret Drew è nata il circa 1425 nel England come figlia di Thomas Drew e Agnes. Lei sposato Walter Samborne circa 1449, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì il 14 febbraio 1495, nel Southcote, Berkshire, England, all'età di 71 anni, e fu sepolta a Greyfriars Church, Reading, Berkshire, England.

ja.Greyfriars Church, Reading, Berkshire, E: イギリスバークシュアイアレアディンググレイフリアルズ チャーチ
ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Drewは約1425年にイギリスでThomas DrewとAgnesの娘として生まれた。約1449年にイギリスでWalter Samborneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼女は1495年2月14日にイギリスバークシュアイアソウスコトで71歳で死去し，イギリスバークシュアイアレアディンググレイフリアルズ チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Drew(은)는 대략 1425년 영국에서 Thomas Drew(와)과 Agnes의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1449년 영국에서 Walter Samborne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1495년 2월 14일 영국 버크실르 소우스컷에서 71세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 버크실르 리딩 그리프리아르스 처르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Margaret Drew nasceu aproximadamente 1425, em Inglaterra filha de Thomas Drew e Agnes. Ela casou-se com Walter Samborne aproximadamente 1449, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ela faleceu em 14 de fevereiro de 1495, em Southcote, Berkshire, Inglaterra, com 71 anos, e foi sepultada em Greyfriars Church, Reading, Berkshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Margaret Drew родилась около 1425 г. в местности Англия. Её родителей звали Thomas Drew и Agnes. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Walter Samborne около 1449 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Она умерла 14 февраля 1495 г. в местности соуткот, биркшир, Англия в возрасте 71 год и была похоронена в местности грифрярс чурч, ридинг, биркшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Margaret Drew nació aproximadamente 1425, en Inglaterra como hija de Thomas Drew y Agnes. Se casó con Walter Samborne aproximadamente 1449, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió el 14 de febrero de 1495, en Southcote, Berkshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 71 años, y fue sepultada en Greyfriars Church, Reading, Berkshire, Inglaterra.
